items.title: Mark Sheridan Waden faces trial over alleged murder of US beauty queen Priscilla Brooten
items.summary: The murder trial of Mark Sheridan Waden has heard allegations he killed his then-partner Priscilla Brooten during an argument almost eight years ago, after the American former beauty queen learned he was having an affair with a colleague.

items.title: Buyers of Wieambilla property tell owner they will respect the lives lost
items.summary: The son of Wieambilla police killers says the new owners of the property are open to maintaining a memorial at the site to the slain officers.

items.title: Euphoria season 3 finale recap: Guns, drugs, the bible and Rue's fate
items.summary: Here's your recap of season 3, episode 8 of Euphoria. There are spoilers. You have been warned.

items.title: Maroons lose Pat Carrigan and Gehamat Shibasaki ahead of State of Origin II against Blues
items.summary: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

items.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over death of mental health patient at RDH
items.summary: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

items.title: International Ebola cases climbing 'fast' but Australia won't impose travel restrictions
items.summary: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

items.title: Teenagers on NSW Central Coast create student-led Northwave music festival
items.summary: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.

items.title: Police to search 1,000 mine shafts, drain dam in renewed search for Trisha Graf
items.summary: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

items.title: Australia wins women's and men's rugby sevens final at World Championship leg in Spain
items.summary: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

items.title: $6 million of cocaine found near burnt-out truck at boat ramp in Whitsundays
items.summary: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

items.title: Tasmania's greyhound racing ban won't go to upper house vote until success assured, premier says
items.summary: A bill to ban greyhound racing in Tasmania has been delayed until the government can secure enough support to pass it. Meanwhile, financial support has been offered to racing participants ahead of the 2029 phase-out deadline.

items.title: Reconciliation Week puts spotlight on Welcome to Country tradition after divisive Anzac Day services
items.summary: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

items.title: Easy and affordable curries to bring some winter warmth
items.summary: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.

items.title: Historic paddle-steamer PS Canally returns to working life with love from volunteers
items.summary: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

items.title: Why Israel's Beaufort Castle seizure is historically and strategically significant
items.summary: Israel has captured a castle in southern Lebanon in a mountainous military siege that marked Israeli forces' deepest incursion into the country in 26 years.

items.title: Chadstone cold case accused Garry Sullivan faces court three decades after armed robbery
items.summary: A 78-year-old man accused of shooting three people in an Armaguard robbery at a busy cinema in 1994 faces court.

items.title: Adelaide Hills man Bo De-Simone pleads guilty to murder of mother Linda Simon
items.summary: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

items.title: Thousands of baby green sea turtles head to sea after successful egg relocation
items.summary: The project at Raine Island, where 90 per cent of northern green sea turtles nest, has been working for a decade to improve hatching success.

items.title: Memory failure contributed to deaths of children left in hot cars, NSW coroner finds
items.summary: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

items.title: Mass flowering of Gebang palms in Darwin described as 'once-in-a-lifetime' natural event
items.summary: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

items.title: Tourism operators say AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north
items.summary: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

items.title: House fire allegedly started by half-brother of murdered teen Angus Beaumont
items.summary: A fire that destroyed a Redcliffe home and damaged two others was allegedly started by the half-brother of murdered teenager Angus Beaumont.

items.title: Alleged Islamic State member Rayann El Houli appears for bail hearing
items.summary: Melbourne woman Rayann El Houli appears in court for a bail hearing after being charged with travelling to Syria to join the Islamic State group, before returning home last year.

items.title: Police officers in Mount Isa shoot dog in street
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment
items.summary: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

items.title: Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's French Open dinner for black players part of 'an old tradition'
items.summary: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

items.title: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, says government is failing on First Nations policy
items.summary: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.

items.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine
items.summary: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

items.title: Antisemitism royal commission rejects federal government's bid to keep cabinet documents confidential
items.summary: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

items.title: Large flock of critically endangered swift parrots seen near Port Macquarie
items.summary: The sighting of critically endangered parrots is seen as "really exciting" after hardly any were seen across NSW last year. But a bird expert warns the species still faces significant threats.

items.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City wins FA Cup, beating Dario Vidošić's Brighton and Hove Albion
items.summary: The Women's FA Cup has a distinctly Australian flavour with Mary Fowler's Manchester City thumping Charlize Rule's Brighton and Hove Albion, coached by compatriot Dario Vidošić, whose father was honoured by fans at Wembley.

items.title: Joao Fonseca reaches first grand slam quarterfinal while Iga Świątek is out of French Open
items.summary: Nineteen-year-old Joao Fonseca beats two-time French Open runner-up Casper Ruud to reach his first grand slam quarterfinal, while four-time champion Iga Świątek suffers her earliest exit since 2019.

items.title: Defence Minister says Australia could still buy five US submarines under AUKUS
items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

items.title: WA storms leave thousands without power as wild weekend weather continues
items.summary: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

items.title: Swifts Creek Bush Nursing Centre after-hours nursing cuts worry residents
items.summary: Swifts Creek is an hour-and-a-half drive from the nearest hospital and residents are worried cuts to after-hours care at its Bush Nursing Centre might leave the community at risk.

items.title: Cattle company inadvertently tipped off Queensland DPI to biosecurity breach
items.summary: A NSW company tipped off authorities to the illegal movement of its cattle, triggering the investigation that led to the largest-ever biosecurity fine under a Queensland law.

items.title: Concerns native forests could be part of Cement Australia's 'sustainable' fuel option as it moves away from coal
items.summary: Used tyres and wood waste will soon help to power a large cement manufacturing site in Tasmania as Cement Australia moves away from coal to alternative fuels, but some remain concerned the environmental benefit may be offset if the wood waste is sourced from logged native forests.

items.title: Natural hydrogen potential like 'tripping over gold', SA minister says
items.summary: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

items.title: Socceroos reveal 26-player squad for 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Recently realigned Cristian Volpato and Japan-born Tete Yengi are in line for potential national debuts after being named in Australia's squad for this year's World Cup.

items.title: Iran weekly briefing: Trump demands changes to peace plan as Israel pushes ahead
items.summary: Donald Trump is reported to be amending a peace plan, which could fuel further delays in finding a final agreement.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Kmart and Shein claim designs Sabo Skirt said they copied were not original
items.summary: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs have been accused of copying are substantially similar to pre-existing designs.

items.title: Women reveal Byron Bay's predatory 2000s culture of rape, humiliation and shame
items.summary: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, when they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls, then publicly humiliate them.

items.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut' of festival takings
items.summary: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.

items.title: Liberal Party quietly publishes paper grappling with party's failure to attract women, young people
items.summary: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.

items.title: Is this the tax reform hill Albanese and Chalmers want to die on?
items.summary: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

items.title: National home values flatline in May for the first time since January 2025
items.summary: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

items.title: A police assault put a man in the ICU. Then came the cover-up
items.summary: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

items.title: How Trials grew up, got sober and confronted his past on debut solo album Hendle
items.summary: The prolific A.B. Original rapper-producer talks about learning to "marry the wrong feeling with the right music".

items.title: Myanmar blast kills dozens in rebel-held village, mining explosives blamed
items.summary: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

items.title: Family lawyers say there's growing interest in 'prenups'. Here's how they work
items.summary: Binding financial agreements take time and money to obtain. Here's how they work.

items.title: This week in history quiz, June 1: Quiz: Will you be crowned the history champ?
items.summary: Who took the first five-wicket haul in one-day cricket? And which state capital was the last to get ABC TV?

items.title: Young Australians reject budget and One Nation popularity climbs further in new poll
items.summary: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

items.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize Beaufort castle
items.summary: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

items.title: Horses bolt through streets of Rome after police officer lights unauthorised fireworks
items.summary: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

items.title: Photos emerge of rescued Laos divers smiling and in good spirits as search continues for two others
items.summary: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

items.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
items.summary: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.

items.title: Tasmania Police 'open' to learning from volunteer group that has found remains in two missing person cases
items.summary: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.

items.title: As Iran's internet comes online after months-long blackout, first posts send anti-regime videos viral
items.summary: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.

items.title: US military strike kills three 'narco-terrorists' in Pacific Ocean, takes overall death toll to 202
items.summary: It is the latest strike in a months-long series of attacks from the US on suspected drug trafficking boats in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Haas laments Broncos' BS as Maguire warns against hoping for another miracle
items.summary: After four straight losses, the 5-7 Broncos are in the same position as last year before launching an audacious run to the premiership, but coach Michael Maguire says the team should not expect to be able to do that again.

items.title: US fires missile into commercial ship trying to reach Iran, Central Command says
items.summary: The US military fires a missile into the engine room of the merchant vessel trying to break through its blockade of Iranian ports, the Central Command says.
